opportunities.rationale: Shopee parent crashed ~24% on 3 Mar after Q4 EPS miss despite revenue beat and heavy investment spend guidance. RSI hit deeply oversold territory. Revenue and EBITDA growth intact; sentiment-driven selling. Stabilising 85-89 zone; hold above 84. Stop below crash low at 78.

MongoDB dropped ~27% after softer FY27 guidance overshadowed a Q4 revenue beat (+27% YoY). Brokers maintained Buy/Overweight with targets $310-325 (Jefferies, JPMorgan). Atlas growth above 30% YoY intact. Entry on stabilisation above 258; invalidated below 242.

BioNTech fell ~17% on 10 Mar on lower COVID-19 vaccine revenue guidance, masking a sizeable oncology and mRNA pipeline. RSI ~28 fits panic-stabilisation pattern. Citi PT $130. Multiple Phase 2/3 oncology readouts in H1 2026 are positive asymmetric catalysts. Stabilisation above 79 required; stop below 70.

Amazon dropped ~9% in Feb on ~$200B capex guide. AWS +19% YoY Q4; re-acceleration to 22-24% is consensus by Q2. Capex shock, not demand deterioration. Mean reversion toward $238 (200-day MA) is the base case. MU earnings March 18 as secondary AI infrastructure read. Invalidated below 197.

LULU is down ~19% YTD to 52-week lows ahead of Q4 earnings March 17 (after close). CEO vacancy, product transition, Elliott pressure. Consensus EPS $4.74; LULU beat in 4 of last 4 quarters (+16.7% beat Q3). Median PT $200. Post-crash entry for a post-earnings bounce ONLY on U.S. comps beat or positive CEO update. Exit if comps miss.

DocuSign fell ~28% from recent high on guidance reset. Q4 FY2026 earnings March 17 (after close). All 10 covering analysts Strong Buy; avg PT $197.80 (~46% upside). Jefferies $225, JPMorgan $230. Require stabilisation above 59 and post-earnings beat confirmation.

General Mills dropped ~14% on 5 Mar on flat organic sales guidance. Q3 FY2026 earnings March 18. Defensive staples recover faster in stagflation; GIS 4.1% dividend provides a floor. RSI hit 27 at the low; now stabilising. Mean reversion toward pre-crash 68 is the base case on a beat.

Williams-Sonoma fell ~18% on cautious FY2026 guidance and tariff headwinds on imported goods. Q4 FY2026 earnings March 18. WSM beaten EPS 7 of last 8 quarters. RSI below 32. Mean reversion toward $175-180 on a Q4 beat. Stop at 133 (below crash low 135).

Carnival fell ~16% on elevated fuel costs and softening European bookings. Q1 FY2026 earnings March 19. LOW conviction: Iran-war macro directly pressures CCL fuel costs and consumer sentiment. Speculative 1% risk position only. Require hold above 22; exit on Brent breaking above $110.

Dollar Tree crashed ~22% on Feb 26 after missing Q4 and guiding to multi-year store restructuring. Q4 FY2025 earnings March 16 (pre-market). In stagflation consumers trade down; discount retail outperforms. RSI bottomed near 20. Post-crash entry requires Q4 to show stabilising traffic; any positive Family Dollar separation update accelerates mean reversion to 84.
